Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given a system log and need to transform it into a process mining event log

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Case Identification We need to group events into coherent cases Looking at the log we see the user working on multiple documents and applications We can consider each distinct document or task as a case

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: There are two Word documents Document1 docx and Quarterly Report docx However note that the user switches between them and does work on each Also there is an Excel file and an email in Chrome

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively we can consider the entire session as one case one work session but then we lose the ability to analyze different tasks

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Another approach we can use the document name or window title as the case identifier

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The instructions say A case can represent a logical unit of user work such as editing a specific document handling a particular email or reviewing a certain PDF

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we can have multiple cases Case 1 Working on Document1 docx Case 2 Handling the email about Annual Meeting Case 3 Reviewing Report Draft pdf

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets look at the sequence of windows Start with Word Quarterly Report docx then Document1 docx then Chrome for email then Adobe Acrobat for Report Draft pdf then Excel for Budget 2024 xlsx then back to Document1 docx then closes it then works on Quarterly Report docx

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can assign the events to the case of the window that the event belongs to For example when the user is in Document1 docx thats case Document1 docx When in Quarterly Report docx thats case Quarterly Report docx Similarly for the others

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The user works on Document1 docx then switches away then comes back So we can consider the entire work on Document1 docx as one case even with breaks

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets define the cases Case 1 Document1 docx Case 2 Quarterly Report docx Case 3 Email Annual Meeting Case 4 Budget 2024 xlsx Case 5 Report Draft pdf

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For SWITCH events we will assign the case of the target window

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If the event has an App and Window then we use the window as the case ID For SWITCH events we have ToApp and ToWindow so we use the ToWindow as the case ID

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to create Activity Names We have to translate the raw actions into meaningful activities

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Raw actions FOCUS TYPING SAVE SWITCH CLICK SCROLL HIGHLIGHT CLOSE

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We will create activity names such as Switch to Window Type Text Save Document Switch Application Open Email Reply to Email Send Email Scroll Highlight Text Close Window

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The CLICK events have specific actions We can use Open Email Reply to Email Send Email

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets build the event log We will create a table with columns Case ID Activity Name Timestamp and optionally the raw action and any extra attributes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now we have an event log with 26 events and 5 cases
